user_content:
I'm so excited to hear that you're considering getting a facial steamer! It's a great addition to any skincare routine, and it can really help to open up your pores and get the most out of your products.

I've heard great things about the Dr. Jart+ Dermaclar Clearing Solution Facial Steamer, and it's a great option to consider. The Neutrogena Facial Steamer is also a popular choice, and it's very affordable.

When it comes to features, I think multiple steam settings are really important. This way, you can customize your experience and adjust the steam to your skin type. A built-in timer is also super helpful, so you don't have to worry about over-steaming your skin.

Remember to always follow the manufacturer's instructions and take necessary precautions when using a facial steamer. And don't forget to follow up with a toner and moisturizer after steaming to lock in hydration and balance your skin's pH.

I think I'll try infusing the bourbon with cardamom and rose petals first. How long do you think I should infuse it for? And do you have any recommendations for the ratio of cardamom to rose petals?

No, I didn't mean the current NBA playoffs, I was referring to the ones that started a few weeks ago when I was watching the Lakers vs Clippers game on April 15th. Anyway, back to golf, yeah, I think I'll take a lesson or two while I'm at Bandon Dunes, it would be great to get some tips on how to navigate the course. And yeah, my family and I usually play golf together on weekends, we really enjoy it.

I think I'll go with the Simple Greens Salad as a side dish. I have some mixed greens and cherry tomatoes at home, and it's easy to prepare. Do you have any recommendations for a vinaigrette dressing, or should I just use a store-bought one?

I'm looking for some inspiration for my next cocktail experiment. By the way, I just hosted a BBQ party today and served a new recipe for a Spicy Mango Margarita, which was a huge hit! I'm thinking of trying out a new twist on a classic drink, maybe something with a unique flavor profile like my margarita. Do you have any suggestions?
Can you show me 3 meals that I can cook with these ingredients? I have only three ingredients - Onion, tomato, and spinach.
What are some natural remedies for treating dark circles under the eyes?

I'm excited to start customizing my daily task list in Habitica. I think it'll really help me stay on top of my tasks and habits. One thing I'm curious about is how I can use Habitica to track my progress over time. Do you know if Habitica has any features that allow me to view my progress and streaks over time?
I'm thinking of also getting a facial steamer to help open up my pores and get the most out of my skincare products. Do you have any recommendations for a good facial steamer?

I like the sound of the Saffron Gin Fizz and the Cardamom-Rose Old Fashioned. For the Old Fashioned, would it be better to infuse the bourbon or rye with the cardamom and rose petals, or to add them as a garnish or mixed in with the bitters?
I'm thinking of making a batch of Pad Thai for dinner tonight. I recently made it last night and it turned out amazing. Do you have any suggestions for other Asian-inspired dishes I could try making?

Speaking of family, I watched the Masters Tournament with my family on April 20th, and we were all thrilled to see Tiger Woods make his amazing comeback. Do you think Bandon Dunes offers any special packages or experiences for families, like private lessons or guided tours?

That sounds really cool, I love the idea of playing a course that's inspired by the classic links courses of Scotland. I've always been fascinated by the history of golf and the old courses in Scotland. By the way, I've been watching a lot of sports lately, especially the NBA playoffs which just started a few weeks ago.
